title: Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Done-for-you proposal and quote follow-up for web design agencies. Chase quietly, close more projects, and stop letting good proposals go cold.
excerpt: A done-for-you follow-up system that chases every web design proposal and quote on schedule, in your voice, so good projects stop going cold.

sections.intro:
Web design agencies lose more revenue to silence than to lost pitches. You send a proposal for a redesign or a new build, the client says they will review it with their team, and then the thread goes quiet. Two weeks later you are not sure whether to nudge again or move on. Meanwhile the next round of discovery calls is already on your calendar, and chasing feels like the lowest priority thing you could do.

The Ayothedoc AI Operating System fixes this by treating proposal and quote follow-up as a standing job inside your business, not a task you have to remember. It watches every proposal you send, tracks the response, and runs a quiet, polite follow-up sequence in your voice until you get a yes, a no, or a clear next step. You stay in control. The system just keeps the thread warm while you focus on the work.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of your AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, summarize, follow up, and report on your behalf. Proposal and quote follow-up draws on the other three layers to do its job:

  • Context: your agency voice, your typical project tiers, your scope boundaries, and how you talk about timelines.
  • Connections: your inbox, CRM or pipeline tool, proposal or quoting tool, and calendar, all reading from live data.
  • Cadence: a recurring schedule that checks for unanswered proposals each weekday and decides whether to nudge, escalate, or close the loop.

If you have not started yet, the 60-Second Lead Engine is the usual entry point. Proposal follow-up is the natural next capability once leads are landing reliably.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, here is the concrete artifact you get and the service level it runs at:

  • Every sent proposal or quote is logged in your pipeline within 5 minutes, with the client name, project type, value, and send date.
  • If the client has not replied in 3 business days, a follow-up draft is queued in your voice referencing the specific project, not a generic template.
  • Follow-ups run on a 3, 7, 14, and 21 day cadence, with the tone softening at each step and a clean breakup message at the end.
  • Replies are detected and the sequence stops automatically, with the thread flagged for your attention with a one line summary of what the client said.
  • A weekly Monday digest lists every open proposal, days since last contact, total pipeline value, and which ones need your judgment.
  • Drafts always wait for your approval on the first install. Once you trust the voice, you can let routine nudges send on their own.
sections.howWeInstall:
We install this the same way we install every AIOS capability, in three phases:

  1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we look at how you currently send proposals, where they live, and where the follow-up breaks down. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we wire the capability into your existing pipeline and inbox, train it on your voice using past proposals and reply threads, and set the cadence rules. You approve the first week of drafts before anything sends.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the capability runs on its schedule, and we add one new automation per week to your AIOS. See plans for what gets added next.
sections.expectedResults:
Grounded in what we typically see for agencies running 6 to 25 active proposals per month:

  • Proposal response rate increases by 20 to 45 percent, mostly from clients who simply forgot to reply.
  • 2 to 6 hours per week returned to the founder or sales lead, depending on volume.
  • 1 to 3 additional projects closed per quarter from threads that would have gone cold.
  • Pipeline visibility improves immediately, since every proposal now has a known status and a next action.

Standing guarantee: your AIOS recovers 40 or more hours a month across the installed capabilities, or we keep working free until it does.
sections.faq:
Will the follow-ups sound like a bot wrote them?

No. The capability is trained on your past proposals and reply threads, so the drafts read like you wrote them on a calm morning. You also review and approve the voice during install before anything goes out.

What happens if a client replies with a question I need to answer myself?

The sequence stops the moment a reply is detected. The thread is flagged for you with a short summary of what the client asked, so you can step in personally without losing context.

Do I need to change my proposal tool or CRM?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and works with the pipeline, inbox, and proposal tool you already use. We connect to what is there rather than asking you to migrate.
